steps.title: Find the scaling factor for a unit increase in w
steps.content:
When $w$ increases by 1, the exponent in $z(w) = (0.819)^{2w}$ increases by 2 (from $2w$ to $2w+2$). So the ratio of consecutive outputs is:
$$\frac{z(w+1)}{z(w)} = \frac{(0.819)^{2w+2}}{(0.819)^{2w}} = (0.819)^2$$

Each increase of 1 in $w$ multiplies $z(w)$ by $(0.819)^2$.

steps.title: Convert the scaling factor to a percentage decrease
steps.content:
Calculate $(0.819)^2 = 0.6708 \approx 0.671$.

This means $z(w)$ retains about $67.1\%$ of its value, so it decreases by:
$$(1 - 0.671) \times 100\% = 32.9\%$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
